title: Spear of Longinus and its direct effect on Eva pilots
display_title: Spear of Longinus and its direct effect on Eva pilots
wikitext:
[]: Spear of Longinus and its direct effect on Eva pilots             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Sun Jan 18, 2015 7:30 am Maybe this has been discussed, but I couldn't find any mention of this on the wiki. However on re-watching the scene where Asuka gets mutilated it struck me as odd that Asuka's hand split into, when before almost nothing really damaged a pilot inside the Eva. Usually examples were light sprouting from the plug-suit, and of course the melding of the angel Armisael in episode 23, but that didn't... wound the pilot per se. In any case, I watched the sequence again and Asuka's also bleeding from her forehead just after she had a replica of the Spear of Longinus through her face. Of course the splitting of her hand and arm were also by a replica of the SoL. So, so far I've to the conclusion that a Spear of Longinus is one of the only things that does DIRECT damage to the Eva Pilots. In all honesty this makes sense as it's supposed to break AT fields and stop Adam and/or Lilith and either weaken or make them regress or entirely destroy them (wait, is that last part... for sure? we only really see them subdue Adam and/or Lilith). That to me makes that lance pretty freaking powerful. However, in the second part of EoE Shinji's hands are impaled by the replica SoLs and he only gets... like a sore spot? What's that all about then? Is this something that was already... blatantly obvious or common knowledge? And is it even true? But if this is what the Spear of Longinus is capable of... why doesn't it stop Third Impact in EoE? :o

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 18, 2015 8:02 am The higher the sync level, the more sympathetic damage -- we'd seen enough examples of the sympathetic damage starting with episode 2, where Shinji has to be told that his own arm is OK, escalating through to episode 18, where we see Rei affected by the sacrificial amputation of EVA-00's arm and strangulation marks on Shinji's neck when EVA-03 is throttling EVA-01.

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Sun Jan 18, 2015 8:10 am Mr. Tines wrote: The higher the sync level, the more sympathetic damage -- we'd seen enough examples of the sympathetic damage starting with episode 2, where Shinji has to be told that his own arm is OK, escalating through to episode 18, where we see Rei affected by the sacrificial amputation of EVA-00's arm and strangulation marks on Shinji's neck when EVA-03 is throttling EVA-01.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 18, 2015 9:06 am At the relevant points, Asuka was on a berserking high(-sync), whereas Shinji was just being a totally useless turnip like he was all day.

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Sun Jan 18, 2015 11:15 am Mr. Tines wrote: At the relevant points, Asuka was on a berserking high(-sync), whereas Shinji was just being a totally useless turnip like he was all day.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jan 18, 2015 11:32 am The problem is not the Spear - it's the synch rate that determines the damage transferred to the pilot. Asuka takes physical damage from the harpies disemboweling Eva-02 later, and they were just using their hands and teeth at that point.

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Sun Jan 18, 2015 12:17 pm UrsusArctos wrote: The problem is not the Spear - it's the synch rate that determines the damage transferred to the pilot. Asuka takes physical damage from the harpies disemboweling Eva-02 later, and they were just using their hands and teeth at that point.

[]:             Quote Post by The Cruel » Sun Jan 18, 2015 1:18 pm Dougurasu wrote: Well, not as I see it. You see if you rewatch the scene there are three instances of damage. 1. She's impaled through the head by a remake spear. 2. She's disemboweled by the harpies. 3. Her arm is spliced in two by a remake spear. (And after that she's impaled several times by the other spears, but we're not shown the end result of that). 1 and 3 show physically her body getting damage. 1 makes her drip blood from her head (first a big gush and then later we see it on her hands). And with 3, well we see her hand ACTUALLY split. In 2 we only see her grabbing her abdomen (and still her head). We don't see her belly get ripped open (to be honest, thank god, but that's not what we're discussing :p), but she DOES experience the pain (like what normally happens during Angel fights). It can't just the sync rate, unless it wobbles. The only thing that's actually different is that in 1 and 3 it's the spear that attacks Unit 2 and in 2 it's the harpies tearing her apart and eating her. We could all say it's artistic license of course, but the spear being able to actually wound Asuka and the harpies can't (only inflict pain, which is probably very nasty anyway and might even be worse) makes a lot of sense to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Sun Jan 18, 2015 1:27 pm The Cruel wrote: Her head? Don't you mean her left eye? Besides her arm, her eye is also bandaged. And maybe if I'm not mistaken, a puddle of blood formed on her abdomen when the MP-Evas ripped the abdomen from her Eva apart.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 18, 2015 1:41 pm The Cruel wrote: Her head? Don't you mean her left eye? Besides her arm, her eye is also bandaged. And maybe if I'm not mistaken, a puddle of blood formed on her abdomen when the MP-Evas ripped the abdomen from her Eva apart.

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Sun Jan 18, 2015 1:56 pm Sachi wrote: Head? Eye? Same difference. :P No need to split hairs. The only blood we see is that gushing from her eye after being impaled. We do, however, see her clutching at her belly as the MP Evas are ripping Unit-02 apart. The pain the pilot experiences is in correlation with their sync ratio. During her last stand, Asuka was displaying a tremendous amount of capability with her Eva, even using her AT field as a weapon, which was unprecedented for an Eva at that time. With that in mind, as well as the roaring sounds we can hear coming from the Eva itself, it's easy to assume that Asuka and her Eva had gone into a berserk-like state while still remaining conscious and in control, making her a force to be reckon with. Her sync ratio was incredibly high, and because of that she also experienced more literal damage on her own body. The reason this is attributed to her sync ratio rather than the Spear is because of the precedent already set in the show; as Tines pointed out, the trend starts as early as episode 02. There's no reason to build a new theory around the Spear when we already have another supported explanation readily available.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 18, 2015 2:02 pm Dougurasu wrote: I'm not dismissing the original explanation at all :o I'm saying that to Asuka at that moment, the Spear had A LOT more impact on her body than the Harpies had. Just that. See it like this: Asuka is at a sync ratio that makes her feel every attack as pain. Then the Spear comes in and does a.. I dunno, +50 hp attack damage on it, and that's enough to ACTUALLY physically wound her. I mean it IS the Spear that is supposed to destroy or weaken Adam and Lilith, why wouldn't it have an EXTRA effect on top of the pain that Asuka already has. I mean you gotta be honest. The harpies don't cause Asuka to bleed (that sounds disgusting... like this entire topic tbh :p), but the Spear does. The original theory doesn't explain this, my theory does. And as an added bonus my theory doesn't disprove the original theory, meaning they can exist next to eachother :o

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Sun Jan 18, 2015 2:06 pm Sachi wrote: I can accept that. The spears, despite being replicas, did have the power to pierce through her AT Field with little effort. Perhaps it was the sheer power of the spears that caused her eye to bleed/arm to split, whereas the harpies tearing her apart was relatively less powerful (though still devastating).

[]:             Quote Post by The Cruel » Sun Jan 18, 2015 2:41 pm Dougurasu wrote: Right! I thought that this theory (or side-theory or whatever) was sort of common knowledge but apparently not really :o So Shinji being given the holy stigmata, he must've had a seriously low sync rate :o Like he only got 10% of the damage to his actual hands.

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Sun Jan 18, 2015 2:47 pm The Cruel wrote: And the wings that Unit 1 got weren't there just like that. The Eva was in it's purest form. And it didn't even matter in what state Shinji was, unless it affects the Red Earth Purification Ceremony. Eva 01 was already a true doomsday device and what happens to the Eva, happens also on the pilot. Thats a absolute exception.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jan 18, 2015 3:55 pm If it really were the replica spears causing those wounds, Shinji's hands would have had holes in them instead of having dark circles form in his palms. Shinji's synch rate is nowhere nearly as high as Asuka's. And Asuka clutching her stomach is because her innards really are being ripped apart, it's just that the plugsuit is holding them in. Similarly with her eye - the spear went right through Eva-02's head, but a hole through the head would have killed Asuka, hence the relatively superficial eye damage. Notice that when Asuka sends Eva-02 into an unpowered berserk state (the highest state of power that an Eva can achieve), the synch is so high that even the plugsuit splits open. In all these cases, it's the synch ratio that matters. The spears don't make one whit of a difference.

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Sun Jan 18, 2015 4:06 pm UrsusArctos wrote: If it really were the replica spears causing those wounds, Shinji's hands would have had holes in them instead of having dark circles form in his palms. Shinji's synch rate is nowhere nearly as high as Asuka's.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jan 18, 2015 6:51 pm Dougurasu wrote: It really WERE replica spears :/ If you don't trust the screencaps go ahead and watch the scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Sun Jan 18, 2015 7:38 pm UrsusArctos wrote: You don't understand. I meant if the replica spears were responsible for causing the exact physical damage to the pilots as they inflicted on the Evas, Shinji's wounds would be far, far worse. I was typing a different sentence and hadn't edited out the "similarly". It was an editing derp on my part. I'm saying that she's actually torn open inside her plugsuit, but the plugsuit itself doesn't tear. Note that when the spear splits Eva-02's arm, Asuka's arm and the plugsuit surrounding it split apart. (And the inside of the arm is filled with a uniform red mass instead of human innards...ugh) Had Asuka's synch been any higher she would have been killed. The reason why she doesn't die initially is because her synch isn't high enough for her to get a hole all the way through her head, and the result is a punctured eye instead. The implement causing the damage doesn't matter, but the synch ratio does.

[]:             Quote Post by The Cruel » Sun Jan 18, 2015 7:43 pm UrsusArctos wrote: And the inside of the arm is filled with a uniform red mass instead of human innards...ugh
